data.title: Nigel Farage Invests £215,000 in Stack BTC, Taking 5.61% Stake
data.description:
UK Reform Party leader Nigel Farage has invested £215,000 in bitcoin treasury company Stack BTC, taking a 5.61% stake.

According to Foresight News, The Guardian reported that Stack BTC’s investments have fallen 15.48% since the company was founded in March this year, resulting in a loss of about £565,000.

The report said Stack BTC’s largest shareholder is Paul Withers, who holds 20.72% and is also the owner of gold dealer Direct Bullion. Direct Bullion previously paid Farage £270,000 for 12 hours of brand promotion.

Farage denied serving as a brand ambassador for Stack BTC, saying he is only a regular shareholder. However, the report noted that he has appeared in a promotional video for the company.

data.title: Fed’s Daly Says Labor Market Has Stabilized as Inflation Rises From Tariffs and Oil Price Shock
data.description:
San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly said the labor market has stabilized, while inflation has risen due to shocks from tariffs and oil prices.

According to Odaily, Daly said oil prices have already retreated and she hopes related pressures will ease.

Daly added that Federal Reserve policy remains in a slightly restrictive stance.

data.title: Fed Rate Hold Seen At 68.5% For July Ahead Of Jobs Data
data.description:
The CME FedWatch tool put the probability of the Federal Reserve keeping interest rates unchanged in July at 68.5% ahead of the nonfarm payrolls release. According to Jin10, the tool showed a 31.5% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point rate hike by July.

For September, the tool put the probability of rates remaining unchanged at 35.7%, with a 49.2% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point hike and a 15.1% probability of a cumulative 50-basis-point hike.

For October, it showed a 27.8% probability of no change, a 46.2% probability of a cumulative 25-basis-point hike, a 22.6% probability of a cumulative 50-basis-point hike, and a 3.3% probability of a cumulative 75-basis-point hike.

data.title: China A-Share Block Trades: Institutions Net Bought BOE A, Dongshan Precision, Star-Net; Net Sold Zhongke Feice, Fuchuang Precision, Shenzhen Kaifa on July 2
data.description: After-hours data showed that on July 2, institutions appeared on the Dragon and Tiger List for 103 A-share stocks, with 60 seeing net institutional buying and 43 seeing net selling, according to 36Kr. The top three net-bought names were BOE A (CNY 1.458 billion), Dongshan Precision (CNY 424 million) and Star-Net (CNY 293 million). The top three net-sold names were Zhongke Feice (CNY 2.506 billion), Fuchuang Precision (CNY 2.017 billion) and Shenzhen Kaifa (CNY 591 million).

data.title: Polygon Founder Sandeep Says POL Supply Turned Net Deflationary in 2026
data.description:
Polygon founder Sandeep said the Polygon network has become net deflationary on a year-to-date basis in 2026, with more POL burned than newly minted. According to ChainCatcher, he posted on X that about 105.2 million POL were newly minted in 2026 year to date, while roughly 107.7 million POL were destroyed through the network’s base-fee burn mechanism.

Sandeep attributed the supply decline mainly to base-fee burns driven by high transaction volumes as Polygon operates as a payments-focused chain.

He also said Polygon’s transaction volume in May exceeded that of other public blockchains, with monthly transactions reaching 198 million.

data.title: A Polymarket Trader Buys $112,000 in Shares Backing Spain to Beat Austria in 2026 World Cup Match
data.description:
A trader identified as “smart money” bought $112,000 worth of shares backing Spain to win a Polymarket prediction event for the “2026 World Cup Round of 16: Spain vs. Austria,” according to monitoring data from PPP’s prediction market tool.

According to Odaily, the address 0x3dfb153c197d4c19d3b31c1ecd2c7b6860eeabaf opened the position at an average price of $0.74 per share, purchasing 150,000 shares, with an unrealized profit of $1,305 at the time of the report.

The Spain vs. Austria knockout match is scheduled for 03:00 (UTC+8) on July 3. The report said Spain has players including Yamal, Torres, and Pino, is ranked third in the world, and has a total squad value of more than 1.2 billion euros. Austria is ranked 23rd in the world, with a total squad value of 245 million euros and an average player age of 28.

The report added that the two teams have played twice in recent years, with each winning once.

data.title: STOCKS | Serenity Says Sivers Semiconductors Plans to Complete Nasdaq Listing Process in Coming Quarters
data.description:
Sivers Semiconductors (SIVE) has disclosed plans to complete its Nasdaq listing process over the coming quarters, according to a post by Serenity.

According to Odaily, Serenity said the market had previously underestimated this signal and described it as a shift from an “evaluation stage” to a more clearly confirmed timeline for the listing process.

Serenity also said SIVE has completed a 700 million Swedish krona capital increase financing round. Serenity added that institutional participation could help provide price support and said the company may have reached a key inflection point for scaled development.

data.title: Jason Huang: NextGen Venture Adds Circle to Watchlist After 14% Stock Drop
data.description:
NextGen Venture (NDV) founder Jason Huang said the firm is taking the opposite approach from its earlier warning on Strategy (MSTR) pullback risk and has added Circle (CRCL) to its watchlist to look for opportunities as market sentiment comes under pressure. According to Odaily, Huang said Circle shares fell about 14% the previous day after multiple financial institutions jointly launched Open USD, which raised market concerns about intensifying competition.

Huang argued that the market misread the situation, saying large institutions entering the space would not necessarily shrink the stablecoin market and could expand it. He said the global stablecoin market capitalization is about $317 billion and described the sector as still in an early growth stage.

He also cited historical examples in which jointly promoted payment and financial projects failed due to misaligned incentives, including Libra, and said such efforts may not change the core competitive landscape over the long term.

Huang said Circle has been included among a small number of U.S.-listed stablecoin-themed stocks that he considers tradable. He added that he has not provided a specific price target or an immediate plan to build a position, but will focus on tracking Circle’s quarterly execution and changes in fundamentals.

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Futures Little Changed as Investors Rotate Out of Momentum Stocks
data.description: S&P 500 Index futures were little changed as of 7:53 a.m. New York time as investors rotated out of the high-flying momentum stocks that powered last quarter’s rally, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Daly Says AI Investment Shock Raises Inflation Concerns
data.description:
Fed Governor Mary C. Daly said an investment shock tied to artificial intelligence has raised concerns about whether it could cause inflation.

According to Jin10, Daly made the remarks in comments focused on how AI-related investment may affect inflation.

data.title: Elon Musk Advances Tesla’s Terafab Chip Project, Report Says
data.description:
Elon Musk is pushing an internal semiconductor manufacturing initiative at Tesla called “Terafab,” a project reportedly valued at $55 billion and described as a cross-company effort involving both Tesla and SpaceX.

According to Odaily, as the project moves forward, expectations have increased that Musk may propose a merger between Tesla and SpaceX.

The report said the two companies are already operating with closer coordination across several key technology areas based on their organizational structures. It added that multiple executives, including Charlie Kuehmann, reportedly hold important roles at both companies, with cross-company management and collaboration mechanisms forming in core technical areas such as materials engineering.

The analysis described Terafab and the integration of personnel and resources across the two companies as part of Musk’s push for a “unified technology stack and manufacturing system,” bringing Tesla and SpaceX closer to a quasi-integrated operating structure in some key links.

data.title: STOCKS | Fundstrat Raises Its Year-End S&P 500 Target to 8,000, Tom Lee Says
data.description:
Fundstrat’s Tom Lee said the firm raised its year-end S&P 500 target to 8,000 from 7,000.

According to NS3.AI, Lee also identified Fundstrat’s preferred market sectors as technology, financials, industrials, small-caps, and energy/basic materials.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Central Banks Buy 41 Tonnes of Gold in May as Prices Fall 11.7% in June
data.description:
Central banks bought a net 41 tonnes of gold in May, even as gold recorded a fourth consecutive monthly loss in June.

According to NS3.AI, World Gold Council data showed gold fell 11.7% in June following a 1.8% decline in May.

The data also indicated that Poland and China led central bank gold purchases during May.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Garrett Jin Says AI Supply-Chain Funds Are Shifting From Memory Chips to Hyperscalers
data.description:
A market structure shift is underway this week, with capital within the AI industry chain being reallocated, according to Garrett Jin, an agent for the “1011 insider whale.” According to Odaily, Jin said the first change is that memory chips are showing signs of a near-term peak.

Jin said Micron’s share price met resistance around $1,250 and pulled back. He added that despite earnings beating expectations, the stock fell on heavy volume, which he described as a typical pattern of weakening after positive news is priced in.

He also said funds have been flowing out of the memory sector. Jin noted that DRAM-related ETFs declined on heavy volume, while South Korea’s SK Hynix and Samsung Electronics also weakened. He cited data showing that over the past two months, foreign investors have withdrawn more than 72.7 billion won from South Korea’s stock market.

Jin said the second change is a shift of funds toward AI hyperscale cloud providers rather than small- and mid-cap AI-themed stocks, naming Google, Microsoft, and Amazon as key beneficiaries.

He said that when the chip sector came under pressure last Friday, GOOG and MSFT stabilized on heavy volume, and that META’s rise on heavy volume this week reinforced the trend.

Jin attributed the rotation to what he called a “token optimization trend,” arguing that as more simple tasks are handled by lower-cost models, value will increasingly concentrate in token-billed cloud services and orchestration layers rather than the base model layer. He said this supports a strategy of focusing on potential catch-up gains in hyperscalers.

data.title: Bad News for LNG Buyers as Qatar’s Export Revival Falters
data.description:
Qatar’s liquefied natural gas export revival is faltering, with fewer gas tankers making the still-risky crossing of the Strait of Hormuz, according to Bloomberg.

Oil is flowing again through Hormuz, but LNG shipping traffic has not rebounded to the same extent.

data.title: STOCKS | Futu Says H1 Hong Kong IPO Subscriptions on Its Platform Near $9.3 Trillion
data.description:
Futu said that in the first half ended June 30, total subscription amounts for 84 Hong Kong IPOs subscribed via its platform were close to $9.3 trillion, according to Ming Pao.

Futu said 70 of the IPOs recorded subscriptions of more than $100 billion each, and 36 exceeded $1 trillion. It added that more than 880,000 investors cumulatively participated in IPO subscriptions, and that its platform accounted for about 47% of the overall Hong Kong IPO subscription amount market-wide.

data.title: STOCKS | MGM China to Buy MGM Asia Pacific From MGM Resorts for $20 Million
data.description:
MGM China Holdings (2282) has agreed with its controlling shareholder MGM Resorts International (MGM) to acquire MGM Asia Pacific for $20 million, funded from internal resources, according to Ming Pao.

The deal is aimed at expanding MGM China into mainland China’s asset-light hotel management business. MGM Asia Pacific’s portfolio includes eight operating hotels across mainland Chinese cities and a pipeline of more than 12 active projects, as well as access to over 1.5 million members of the “MGM Lion” loyalty program.

data.title: San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly to Attend Meeting on Spain’s Economy
data.description:
San Francisco Fed President Mary C. Daly, a 2027 FOMC voting member, was set to attend a meeting on Spain’s economy in 10 minutes.

According to Jin10, the meeting was scheduled to begin shortly.

data.title: Hyperliquid Trader Loracle Increases ZEC Long Position to $21.77 Million
data.description:
A well-known trader on Hyperliquid, Loracle, increased a leveraged long position in ZEC by $2.26 million, bringing the total ZEC long exposure to $21.77 million. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, monitoring by Hyperinsight showed the position was opened at an average price of $362.28 and had an unrealized profit of $3.81 million as of July 2.

Loracle is described as an early contributor to Hyperliquid and the founder of Hypurrfun. The report also noted that Loracle previously recorded monthly profits of $16 million and has at times been the largest long and short holder of HYPE, often maintaining positions ranging from tens of millions to the hundred-million level.

data.title: WTI Crude Falls 1% Intraday to $67.34 a Barrel
data.description: WTI crude oil fell 1% intraday to $67.34 a barrel. According to Jin10, the price was quoted at $67.34 per barrel after the decline.
